title: Lovász Conjecture
statement_text: Every sufficiently large connected vertex-transitive graph admits a Hamiltonian path.
context_text: Lovász conjectured in 1969 that all sufficiently large connected vertex-transitive graphs are Hamiltonian-connected. The paper observes that any upper bound on $P(n)$ (the worst-case longest path transversal size) yields a lower bound on the longest path length in vertex-transitive graphs, making progress on $P(n)$ directly applicable here.
paper_contribution: Shows that every connected vertex-transitive graph of order $n$ contains a path of length $\Omega(n^{9/14})$, improving the previous best bound of $\Omega(n^{13/21})$.
notes: Stated in prose in the introduction; no labelled conjecture environment visible. Reference [27] is Lovász 1969 (Proc. Calgary Internat. Conf.).
paper_title: Small hitting sets for longest paths and cycles

title: Thomassen Conjecture
statement_text: Every sufficiently large connected vertex-transitive graph admits a Hamiltonian cycle.
context_text: Thomassen conjectured in 1978 that all sufficiently large connected vertex-transitive graphs contain a Hamiltonian cycle. Via the bound $\mathrm{lct}(G) \leq \sqrt{8n}$ for 2-connected graphs and the analogous path-length conversion, the paper translates improvements on $C(n)$ into long-cycle guarantees for vertex-transitive graphs.
paper_contribution: Shows that every connected vertex-transitive graph of order $n$ contains a cycle of length $\Omega(n^{9/14})$, improving the previous best bound of $\Omega(n^{13/21})$.
notes: Attributed via 'cf. [4]' (Barát and Kriesell, Discrete Math. 2010) in the paper; conjecture itself dates to 1978. Stated in prose in the introduction; no labelled conjecture environment visible.
paper_title: Small hitting sets for longest paths and cycles

title: Walther–Zamfirescu Question
statement_text: Does there exist an absolute constant $c > 0$ such that every connected graph $G$ satisfies $\mathrm{lpt}(G) \leq c$?
context_text: Walther and Zamfirescu independently raised whether the minimum longest path transversal size is bounded by a universal constant. Despite the smallest known construction with $\mathrm{lpt}(G) = 3$ being due to Zamfirescu and no example with $\mathrm{lpt}(G) > 3$ having been found in over 50 years, the question remains widely open. The paper studies the growth of the worst-case functions $P(n)$ and $C(n)$ as a relaxation.
paper_contribution: Proves that every connected $n$-vertex graph satisfies $\mathrm{lpt}(G) \leq \sqrt{8n}$ and $\mathrm{lpt}(G) \leq O(\ell^{5/9})$ where $\ell$ is the length of a longest path, improving the previous state-of-the-art bound of $O(n^{2/3})$.
notes: Stated in prose in the introduction as 'raised independently by Walther and Zamfirescu [36]'; reference [36] is Zamfirescu, J. Combinatorial Theory Ser. B, 1972. No labelled question environment visible.
paper_title: Small hitting sets for longest paths and cycles
